sections.category: LLM Workflow > OpenClaw / NemoClaw / Hermes Agents

bullets.body: **Hermes Agent v0.11.0 "Interface" (Apr 23) — adoption Apr 26-28; Ink-based React TUI, native AWS Bedrock, GPT-5.5 via Codex OAuth, 1,556 commits / 761 PRs / 224K insertions / 29 contributors** — Largest Hermes Agent release to date carries forward. Pluggable transport architecture under every provider; 5 new inference paths; 17th messaging platform (QQBot); shell hooks (pre/post tool call, on_session_start); webhook direct-delivery zero-LLM mode; configurable subagent orchestrator with file-coordination layer.

citations.label: GitHub - NousResearch/hermes-agent v0.11.0 RELEASE notes

bullets.body: **OpenClaw v2026.4.25-beta — sub-24h cadence persists into Apr 28** — Voice-stack hardening, browser-automation safe-tab URLs, CDP-native role-snapshot fallback, CLI image-gen `--background`, Discord voice channel `channels.discord.voice.model` override, plugin-onboarding install-record preservation. The cadence is the story; no new minor by Apr 28.

citations.label: OpenClaw - Latest Features & Release Notes

bullets.body: **Multica v0.2.16 (Apr 24) — Chat V2 dedicated sidebar + main-area page, right-click context menus on issues with unified actions across list/board/detail, in-app feedback flow, redesigned Autopilot modal; v0.2.8 (Apr 20) added Kimi CLI as a runtime over ACP + per-agent `model` field with provider-aware dropdown** — Open-source managed-agents orchestrator that turns Claude Code / Codex / OpenClaw / OpenCode / Hermes / Gemini / Pi / Cursor Agent into assignable teammates: assign issues, agent picks up the work, writes code, reports blockers, updates statuses autonomously. Vendor-neutral, self-hosted; sits alongside AgentCore Managed Harness and Claude Managed Agents in the orchestration layer.

citations.label: GitHub - multica-ai/multica releases

sections.category: LLM Workflow > OpenAI / Microsoft / Anthropic — Apr 27 partnership re-architecture

bullets.body: **OpenAI–Microsoft AGI clause killed Apr 27 — non-exclusive license through 2032, revenue share capped at 2030, AWS $38B already signed (Nov 2025)** — Microsoft's commercial IP rights to OpenAI no longer terminated by an OpenAI-board AGI declaration; revenue share now ends on a calendar (2030); Microsoft's stake (~$135B / ~27% as-converted) intact. OpenAI now legally clear to ship its full product line on AWS, Oracle, Google.

**Simon Willison Apr 27: "Tracking the history of the now-deceased OpenAI Microsoft AGI clause"** — The canonical practitioner-history primer of how the clause was structured and what specifically the Apr 27 amendment removed.

citations.label: Simon Willison - Tracking the history of the now-deceased OpenAI Microsoft AGI clause

bullets.body: **AWS Weekly Roundup Apr 27 — Anthropic + Meta partnerships, Lambda S3 Files, AgentCore CLI** — The single most content-dense AWS post in April: deepened Anthropic collab (Trainium / Graviton silicon level), Meta Graviton-at-scale deployment, Claude Cowork in Bedrock, AgentCore managed harness + CLI + Skills, plus Lambda Files (in-Lambda S3 access) and SES inbound improvements.

citations.label: AWS Blog - AWS Weekly Roundup: Anthropic & Meta partnership, AWS Lambda S3 Files, Amazon Bedrock AgentCore CLI, and more

bullets.body: **Claude Cowork in Amazon Bedrock — enterprise team collaboration with AWS-resident data** — Cowork (Anthropic's collaborative-AI surface) now deploys inside existing Bedrock environments without leaving AWS data residency.

citations.label: AWS ML Blog - Running Claude Cowork in Amazon Bedrock

bullets.body: **Microsoft VibeVoice — open-source frontier voice AI (MIT licensed; in Transformers since Mar 6, fresh practitioner attention Apr 27)** — Whisper-style STT with built-in speaker diarization, 50+ languages, long-form (60-minute pass) transcription producing structured Who/When/What output.

citations.label: GitHub - microsoft/VibeVoice: Open-Source Frontier Voice AI

bullets.body: **Simon Willison Apr 28: Introducing `talkie-1930-13b-base` — 13B vintage language model from Nick Levine, David Duvenaud, Alec Radford, trained on 260B tokens of pre-1931 historical text** — A first-of-its-kind "vintage" LM release; pre-1931 corpus avoids contemporary contamination.

citations.label: Simon Willison - Introducing talkie: a 13B vintage language model from 1930

bullets.body: **vLLM v0.20 path: Transformers v5 compatibility-fix wave continues; `Update to transformers v5` (PR #30566) primary integration vehicle** — `huggingface-hub>=5` updates land; `transformers>=5` model-loader compatibility fixes across multiple architectures; `protobuf` patch for CVE-2026-0994; deprecations dropped (BitBlas quant, Marlin 24, `reasoning_content`, deprecated pooling items, `VLLM_ALL2ALL_BACKEND` env). Speculative-decoding maturity + MoE routing for Gemma 4 / DeepSeek-V3-style models continues.

citations.label: GitHub - vllm-project/vllm: Update to transformers v5 (#30566)

bullets.body: **Transformers v5 itself (Hugging Face Apr 2026): "Simple model definitions powering the AI ecosystem"** — The canonical Hugging Face write-up framing the v5 break + its inference-server consumers (vLLM / SGLang / TGI).

citations.label: Hugging Face Blog - Transformers v5: Simple model definitions powering the AI ecosystem

bullets.body: **SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) — patch still not upstream into Apr 28; copy-paste class widening to vLLM (CVE-2025-61620) / LMDeploy / adjacent servers** — Coordinated disclosure window has closed without `ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment` mitigation; v0.5.10 ships exploitable. PoC `github.com/Stuub/SGLang-0.5.9-RCE` continues to circulate; CERT/CC VU#915947 active.

citations.label: Feedly - CVE-2026-5760 / 9.8 / exploit + patch (no upstream fix)

bullets.body: **AgentCore Managed Harness (4-region preview), CLI (14-region GA), Skills on Kiro Power — Apr 27 + filesystem-persistence preview** — Define-an-agent (model + system prompt + tools), per-session microVM, mid-session model swap, filesystem persistence for resumable sessions; CLI runs CDK today, Terraform soon.

citations.label: The NAS Guy - AWS Weekly Roundup Apr 27 2026 (mirror)

bullets.body: **DeepSeek V4-Pro / V4-Flash continue traction Apr 26-28 — Hugging Face V4-Pro page live; Pro = 1.6T MoE / 49B active; Flash = 284B / 13B active; 1M context** — Hybrid Attention reduces compute to 27% (Pro) / 10% (Flash) of V3.2 in 1M-context. Pro priced at $1.74 / $3.48 per M tokens. Apr 24 official preview status carries through Apr 28.

citations.label: Felloai - DeepSeek V4 Released: Everything You Need to Know (April 2026)

sections.category: Local LLM > Qwen / Tencent / Other Chinese LLMs

bullets.body: **Qwen3.6-Max-Preview (Apr 20, traction Apr 26-28) — Alibaba's most-capable LM; SWE-bench Pro / SkillsBench / SciCode top scores; 260K-token context; preserve_thinking mode for agentic workflows** — Anchors the Qwen3.6 family alongside Qwen3.6-27B (Apr 21, dense 27B / 262K context) and Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (Apr 16, Apache 2.0). Qwen3.6-Plus also in market for enterprise agentic deployment.

citations.label: Qwen.ai - Qwen3.6-Max-Preview: Smarter, Sharper, Still Evolving

bullets.body: **PrismML 1-bit Bonsai-8B carries forward Apr 26-28 — iPhone 17 Pro Max 44 tok/s, M4 Pro Mac 131 tok/s, RTX 4090 368 tok/s; 1.15 GB total file** — Native 1-bit training (not PTQ); 14x size reduction; 4-5x energy-per-word reduction; CUDA / Metal / Android / CPU. Cross-stack on-device deployment baseline.

citations.label: PrismML - Bonsai 8B repo / model card

bullets.body: **Unsloth v0.1.37-beta (Apr 23) — full Studio UI/UX overhaul, Preserve Thinking toggle for Qwen3.6, gpt-oss thinking toggle, Linux CUDA uses latest llama.cpp prebuilt, Kimi-K2.6 enabled, experimental API support** — Studio gains collapsible sidebar, chat deletion + past-conversation search, expanded Settings page (no more entering HF tokens twice). Cumulative on v0.1.36-beta (Apr 8): Gemma 4 training bug fixes; **Gemma-4-E2B local-train on 8 GB VRAM, ~1.5x faster, ~60% less VRAM than FA2**. Apr 11 added MiniMax-M2.7 + updated Gemma 4 GGUFs to Google's official chat template. Belongs alongside Bonsai-8B as the on-device-fine-tuning baseline.

citations.label: GitHub - unslothai/unsloth releases

bullets.body: **Unsloth Updates / changelog — official tracker for the Apr 8 / 11 / 23 cadence** — Reference page for the consumer-GPU fine-tuning + Studio rollout that's been the loudest local-trainer narrative in April.

citations.label: Unsloth Documentation - Updates / Changelog

bullets.body: **llama.cpp build cadence continues Apr 27-28 — macOS ARM64/x64, Android, OpenEuler, CUDA binaries Apr 27; llama-cpp-python 0.3.21 same-day** — Sub-day build train holds; minimalist web-UI for local model operations announced as alternative to server-based interaction.

citations.label: PyPI - llama-cpp-python (Apr 27, 2026 release 0.3.21)

bullets.body: **Ollama v0.21.3-rc0 (Apr 24) — `max` think-parameter + improved OpenAI response mapping for reasoning effort** — Builds on v0.21.2 (Apr 23) Kimi K2.6 + Gemma 4 MLX path. Native OpenClaw web-search plugin remains bundled.

citations.label: Release Alert - ollama/ollama Releases tracker

bullets.body: **Foundry Local — GA carry-forward (Apr 9 launch); MAI-Transcribe-1 + GPT-4.1 model graders + RFT Global Training (o4-mini) continue Apr 26-28** — Cross-platform local AI on Windows / macOS / Android / on-prem Azure Local. MLX support remains an open issue (`microsoft/Foundry-Local#329`).

sections.category: AI Security > Solutions: Zero-Trust Access Control for Agentic AI

bullets.body: **Okta for AI Agents — GA Apr 30 (countdown: 2 days from Apr 28)** — Agent Discovery & Governance, Agent Gateway as central MCP control plane (virtual MCP server aggregating Okta MCP registry), credential vaulting + auto-rotation, runtime enforcement via universal logout. Prior coverage carries forward into the Apr 30 GA event.

citations.label: Cloud Security Alliance - ATF: Zero Trust for AI Agents

bullets.body: **Microsoft "Zero Trust for AI" (RSAC 2026 carry-forward) — new AI pillar in Zero Trust Workshop, updated Data + Networking pillars in Zero Trust Assessment, Zero Trust reference architecture for AI** — Cross-vendor consensus from RSAC 2026 (Cisco's "action control," CrowdStrike, Splunk) reinforces zero-trust-for-agents as the assumed baseline into Apr 28.

citations.label: Cisco - Zero trust for the agentic AI workforce (carry-forward into Apr 28)

sections.category: AI Security > Solutions: Monitor and Manage AI Agent Behavior

bullets.body: **Google + Forcepoint indirect-prompt-injection in-the-wild reports (Apr 24-27) — 10 fresh IPI payloads, including a fully-specified PayPal transaction targeting payment-capable agents and Stripe-donation routing via meta-tag namespace injection** — Indirect injection now >80% of enterprise prompt-injection volume; impacts any agent that browses + summarizes, indexes for RAG, processes metadata/HTML comments, or moderates ad content.

citations.label: Airia - AI Security in 2026: Prompt Injection, the Lethal Trifecta, and How to Defend

bullets.body: **OpenAI publicly concedes prompt-injection class is permanent — VentureBeat Apr 26-27 coverage** — Defenders lag; threat-class moves from "patchable" to "design-constraint." Frames the OWASP LLM Top-10 v2026 promotion thesis.

citations.label: VentureBeat - OpenAI admits prompt injection is here to stay as enterprises lag on defenses

bullets.body: **AMD ATH $352.99 Apr 24; +65% MTD into May 5 print; consensus $9.84B revenue / +33% YoY EPS** — AI Chip Challenger framing solidifies through Apr 28; 37 Buy/Strong Buy / 12 Hold / 0 Sell. Carry-forward into May 5.

**Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan: "the CPU is reinserting itself as the indispensable foundation of the AI era" (investor call carry-forward Apr 27)** — CPUs entering the AI conversation as the inference + day-to-day workload primitive next to GPUs.

**Cerebras IPO mid-May window confirmed, $22B-$25B target — $20B+ / 750MW OpenAI deal through 2028; underwriters Morgan Stanley / Citi / Barclays / UBS** — 2025 revenue $510M (+76% YoY), $87.9M net income; $24.6B remaining performance obligations (15% recognized 2026/2027). 24% of 2025 revenue from G42 / 62% from MBZUAI.

**Mag 7 super-week Apr 28-30 — MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN/META Apr 29 after close, AAPL Apr 30 after close; ~$700B 2026 capex aggregated; Q1 expected +20.3% earnings on +22% revenue** — All four Wednesday names up >10% MoM into prints; AAPL +6%.
